summary: Auth-config reads and updates must prove legacy-cache removal, reject every protected redirect, validate request fields against the audited schema, expose only explicitly audited scalar metadata, and keep credential inputs out of CLI arguments and MCP calls.
reason: Supabase AuthConfigResponse contains provider secrets, SMTP credentials, hook signing material, SMS credentials, and credential-capable Auth Hook URIs; filtering after serialization can leak those values to stdout, files, webhooks, MCP tools, or world-readable cache files.
validated_outcome: Synthetic credential sentinels are absent from all output and error paths; protected GET/PATCH operations fail closed unless legacy HTTP caches are removed and never cache responses; every redirect from a protected request is denied without forwarding authorization; unknown live and dry-run request fields fail identically; credential-capable Auth Hook URIs are omitted from responses; secret-valued flags and all present or future auth-config writes through MCP fail closed with stdin guidance.
